changes_summary: Revised the plan to close all significant critique gaps: it now updates the caller-uploaded image source contract, rejects epicless attachments explicitly, adds a local blob adapter for SQLite CLI attachments, includes full attached_image schema/tool/stream plumbing, and specifies BlobRef-based resident send_image file posting.

success_criteria.criterion: Project metadata declares Python 3.12+ support and preserves the canonical `test` optional dependency extra.
`caller_uploaded` is accepted consistently by image storage constraints, tool schemas, and tests while existing `user_uploaded` Discord behavior still works.
SQLite CLI invocation has a concrete local blob adapter path, and `arnold turn --attach <image>` works end to end with an explicit epic id.

Python `run_turn(..., attachments=...)` supports image attachments from `Path`, `bytes`, and `(bytes, mime_type)`, creates `caller_uploaded` image rows before model execution, and exposes them through `list_images`.
Invocation attachments with `epic_id=None` fail with an explicit `attachments_require_epic` envelope error and do not create orphan image/blob state.

Resident-mode `send_image` constructs a `BlobRef`, fetches image bytes through `Blob.get`, posts actual file payloads through the Discord transport, updates the outbound message row with the confirmed Discord id, and settles the Discord external request ledger row.
Invocation-mode `send_image` preserves audited `tool_call` rows/events and also emits first-class `attached_image` events that validate against `agent_kit/envelope.schema.json`.
`--stream-events` and Python `on_event` callbacks emit the same `attached_image` events captured in the final envelope.
`set_typing` is registered as a tool; resident mode calls transport typing behavior and invocation mode succeeds as a no-op/audited tool call.
Whitelist rejection, status-message non-persistence, outbound message persistence, and status lifecycle behavior remain aligned with the spec and covered by regression tests.
The broader FastAPI/asyncpg architecture migration is not implemented in this tranche.
All touched focused tests pass before running the full suite.
The full test suite passes after the tranche is complete, or any failure is clearly unrelated to touched behavior and documented.
Attachment normalization and `LocalBlobStore` remain small and direct, with no broad storage or transport rewrite beyond the requested parity tranche.
New tests are focused on acceptance criteria rather than broad snapshots that would make unrelated future changes noisy.

assumptions: The current SQLite/local invocation and Discord resident architecture remains the implementation target; FastAPI and asyncpg stay deferred.
Invocation attachment parity for this tranche is image-only.
Attachments require an existing `epic_id` because image rows and blob paths are epic-scoped; invocation without `--epic` remains supported only when no attachments are provided.
`caller_uploaded` should be added as a first-class image source while preserving existing `user_uploaded` Discord behavior.
A small filesystem `LocalBlobStore` is acceptable because it is the minimal missing port implementation needed for end-to-end SQLite CLI attachments.
First-class `attached_image` events are preferred and will be implemented only with matching dataclass, JSON schema, tool/event streaming, and tests.
The existing optional dependency extra `test` remains canonical; any `test-extra` alias must be additive and non-breaking.
Existing dirty worktree changes, if any, are unrelated and must not be reverted.
